# Fixture answers for scripts/gather-requirements.sh
# System: Task Tracker — a REST API + web app for personal task management
# Each line is one stdin read() call in the script.
# Blank lines represent pressing Enter (default / stop-loop).
# Lines beginning with # are NOT part of the fixture — they are comments for humans.
# To use: grep -v '^#' requirements-answers.txt | gather-requirements.sh

# §01 Purpose and Scope
Task Tracker
A personal task management platform for creating, organising, and tracking tasks with deadlines and tags.
REST API, web frontend, user authentication, task CRUD, email notifications, admin console
Mobile native apps, billing and payment processing

# §02 Stakeholders — 3 stakeholders then stop
End User
General public
Create and manage tasks efficiently to stay organised
High
y
System Administrator
Operations team
Monitor system health and manage user accounts
Medium
y
Product Owner
Management
System meets business goals and is delivered on schedule
High

# §03 Business Requirements — 2 goals then stop
Users can manage their tasks end-to-end from creation to completion
Registered users have created, updated, and completed at least one task in their first session
M
y
System is reliably available during working hours
Less than 1 hour unplanned downtime per calendar month
M

# §04 Functional areas — 3 areas
User Authentication

# Stories for User Authentication — 3 then stop
new user
register with email and password
access the system
M
y
registered user
log in with valid credentials
view my task list
M
y
registered user
reset my password via a link sent to my email
regain access if locked out
S

# More areas?
y
Task Management

# Stories for Task Management — 3 then stop
user
create a task with a title, description, and due date
track what I need to do
M
y
user
mark a task as complete
record my progress
M
y
user
filter my tasks by status and due date
focus on what needs attention next
S

# More areas?
y
Notifications

# Stories for Notifications — 1 then stop
user
receive an email reminder 24 hours before a task is due
avoid missing deadlines
S

# More areas?


# §05 NFRs — 4 then stop
Performance Efficiency
API response time under normal load
p95 response time <= 300ms under 100 concurrent users
M
y
Reliability
System availability
99.5% uptime measured over a rolling 30-day window
M
y
Security
Data protection in transit and at rest
All passwords hashed with bcrypt, all data in transit over TLS 1.3
M
y
Usability
Accessibility compliance
WCAG 2.1 AA verified by automated axe-core scan on every release
S


# §06 Technical Constraints — 2 then stop
Must deploy to AWS
Existing AWS organisation account is the only approved cloud provider
y
Must use PostgreSQL for relational data
Organisational standard database; existing DBA expertise


# §06 Organisational Constraints — 2 then stop
Must comply with GDPR
EU users' personal data is in scope; DPO sign-off required
y
Initial release by Q3 2026
Contractual commitment to early adopters


# §07 Assumptions and Dependencies — 2 entries then stop
Assumption
SendGrid email API supports the webhook events required for delivery confirmation
Email notification feature must be descoped if assumption is wrong
y
Dependency
SendGrid transactional email API
Transactional email for notifications and password reset; fallback is in-app notifications only


# §08 Acceptance Criteria — 3 then stop
FR-01
User is on the registration page and has not previously registered
User submits a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters including one number
Account is created and user receives a confirmation email within 60 seconds
Test
y
FR-02
User is on the login page with a confirmed account
User submits valid credentials
User is redirected to the task list within 2 seconds and a valid JWT session is active
Test
y
NFR-01
System is running under normal conditions with 100 concurrent authenticated users
Any user submits an authenticated API request
p95 response time is <=300ms as measured by a k6 load test
Analysis


# §09 RTM — 7 entries then stop
FR-01
User registration
§1, §6
y
FR-02
User login
§1, §6
y
FR-03
Password reset
§1, §6
y
FR-04
Create task
§1, §5, §6
y
FR-05
Complete task
§1, §6
y
FR-06
Filter tasks
§1, §5, §6
y
FR-07
Email reminders
§1, §6, §7
y
NFR-01
API performance
§1, §10
y
NFR-02
System availability
§1, §10, §11
y
NFR-03
Data security
§1, §8, §10
y
NFR-04
Accessibility
§1, §10
